vulnerability.title: Uninstall gssproxy Package
vulnerability.description: The gssproxy package can be removed with the following command: $ sudo dnf remove gssproxy
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # CAUTION: This remediation script will remove gssproxy # from the system, and may remove any packages # that depend on gssproxy. Execute this # remediation AFTER testing on a non-production # system! if rpm -q --quiet "gssproxy" ; then dnf remove -y --noautoremove "gssproxy" fi

triage_decision.reason: Removing the gssproxy package is a low‑risk operation that does not alter authentication configuration directly; it only uninstalls a optional component and may affect dependent packages, but this can be safely tested and rolled back.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; networking affected; Potential removal of packages that depend on gssproxy

previous_attempts.scan_output: Single-rule scan failed for openscap_012 (rule=xccdf_org.ssgproject.content_rule_package_gssproxy_removed). Could not verify.

review_feedback: Review+QA approved but the verification scan failed. Try a different approach.

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: The remediation plan correctly identifies the need to uninstall gssproxy but has not been executed; ensure the removal command is run and verify the package is gone after testing.
concerns: Potential dependency impact on services that rely on gssproxy
Risk of automatic reinstallation during updates
No explicit testing or rollback verification performed
suggested_improvements: Run the removal command in a test environment first
Add a post‑removal verification step (e.g., rpm -q gssproxy)
Consider using dnf remove -y gssproxy --assumeyes and review dependent packages with dnf repoquery --requires gssproxy
Document a rollback plan in case a dependent service fails
